BrandStory.title: Our Heritage
BrandStory.subtitle: From Rongchang, one of China's four renowned purple clay regions

features.title: Centuries of Ceramic Expertise
features.desc: Rongchang is China's earliest producer of 1000L large clay amphoras, the designated supplier for top Chinese distilleries.

features.title: Optimal Iron Content
features.desc: Our clay contains 7-8% iron, providing essential catalytic properties for wine development while preserving fruit aromas.

features.title: Regular Material Testing
features.desc: Periodic inspections ensure zero heavy metals in our clay, guaranteeing wine safety and quality.

features.title: Controlled Micro-Oxygenation
features.desc: We customize oxygen permeability for different wines, protecting and developing superior fruit characteristics.

craftsmanship.title: Craftsmanship Process
craftsmanship.subtitle: A perfect blend of traditional artistry and modern technology

items.title: Triple Protection Structure
items.desc: Our proprietary top structure features a one-way valve, nitrogen protection, and a unique zero-oxygen water seal, delivering significantly longer freshness than conventional amphoras.

items.title: Large Opening Design
items.desc: Our larger discharge port simplifies fermentation operations. The enhanced ventilation effect allows interior moisture to dry within 2 hours, versus 48+ hours for conventional small-mouth designs, preventing bacterial growth.

items.title: Burgundy-Style Solution
items.desc: For white wines, select amphoras include automatic lees stirring. Combined with our oak stave program and suitable raw materials, achieve Burgundy-style white wines.

items.title: Mature Oak Program
items.desc: Our refined oak stave aging approach preserves abundant fruit aromas while imparting oak barrel-like complexity. Oak staves can be removed without racking, preventing excessive oak flavor.

noLeak.title: Zero Leakage Guarantee
noLeak.desc: Our unique surface treatment ensures zero leakage, preventing wine residue stains from forming on the amphora surface.

traditional.title: Traditional Clay Amphora Limitations
items: Poor sealing leads to excess air, risking oxidation and volatile acidity increase
Water-sealed amphoras cannot prevent air ingress from temperature-driven breathing
Inconvenient residue removal, clarification, and cleaning during fermentation
No temperature control, risking excessive higher alcohols from high fermentation temps
No base platform for easy mobility
Fired below 900°C, low strength, requiring external cement reinforcement

ours.title: Chengjing Advantages
items: Dual-purpose fermentation and storage, maximizing winery space
Elegant purple-red tone transforms industrial equipment into winery aesthetics
Enables small-batch premium varietal production for discerning customers
Excellent oxygenation control combined with natural catalytic properties preserves maximum fruit aroma and refines tannin structure
Fired at 1150-1180°C, high strength with acid-resistant structure from iron mineral fusion with silicates

Craftsmanship.title: Pottery Is Our Tradition
Craftsmanship.subtitle: Pottery Making is Our Tradition
paragraphs: Crafted in China, home to thousands of years of ceramic history, using locally-sourced special natural clay fired at high temperatures.
Due to the unique nature of ceramic, the forming process requires extensive handcraftsmanship.
At high temperatures, organic matter in the clay volatilizes, creating open pores that give our amphoras their characteristic breathability.
